zh.Lands of Rumanoch, Houston, Renfrewshire: 蘇格蘭雷福福爾休斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston是Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston的兒子，於1205年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾休斯頓。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1296年在以色列享年91歲。

zh-hans.Lands of Rumanoch, Houston, Renfrewshire: 苏格兰雷福福爾休斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston是Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston的儿子，于1205年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾休斯頓。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1296年在以色列享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Houston (Renfrewshire), from the genitive case of the personal name Hugh + Middle English, Older Scots ton ‘town, village, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’). The landlord in question was a certain Hugo de Paduinan, who held the place c. 1160. The Scottish surname is also common in Ulster.
Irish: variant of McCutcheon from Mac Uisdein or Mac Uistein.

History: In 1836 the newly founded city of Houston, TX, was named in honor of Sam Houston (1793–1863), soldier and statesman. As commander in chief of the Texan army he achieved Texas independence from Mexico by routing the army of the Mexican general Santa Anna. His ancestors were Ulster Scots who had emigrated to Philadelphia, PA, in the 18th century.

variants.name: Hueston
meanings: English (Middlesex) and Irish: variant of   Houston  .

variants.name: Heuston
meanings: Irish: variant of   Houston   or   Hewson  .

variants.name: Hoston
meanings: English: probably a variant of northern Irish   Houston  .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

variants.name: Hughston
meanings: Scottish: apparently a habitational name from somewhere in Britain, most probably a variant of Houston . There is also a small place called Hughston in the Highland region of Scotland.

variants.name: Huston
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Derry and Antrim): variant of   Houston  .

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston was born in 1205, in Houston, Renfrewshire, Scotland as the son of Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1296, in Israel, at the age of 91.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston est né en 1205, à Houston, Renfrewshire, Écosse, fils de Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1296, à Israël, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston wurde 1205 in Houston, Renfrewshire, Scotland, als Sohn von Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1296 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Israel.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston è nato il nel 1205 nel Houston, Renfrewshire, Scotland come figlio di Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1296, nel Israele, all'età di 91 anni.

ja.Lands of Rumanoch, Houston, Renfrewshire: スコットランドレンフリューシュアイアホーウストン
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houstonは1205年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアホーウストンでSir Hugh FitzReginald de Houstonの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1296年にイスラエルで91歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston(은)는 1205년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 허스턴에서 Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1296년 이스라엘에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston nasceu em 1205, em Houston, Renfrewshire, Scotland filho de Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1296, em Israel, com 91 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston родился в 1205 г. в местности хоустон, ренфрюшир, Шотландия. Его отца звали Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1296 г. в местности Израиль в возрасте 91 год.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Houston nació en 1205, en Houston, Renfrewshire, Scotland como hijo de Sir Hugh FitzReginald de Houston. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1296, en Israel, a la edad de 91 años.
